GPT 5.4 is now shockingly good at reviewing slides, as I discovered last night.

It feels like a detailed teardown from an expert in your field.

You’ll get specific edits on each slide + a broader view on how to frame your narrative better.

It’s hard to describe - just try it!

Sharing part of the response I got (redacted). 

I literally just uploaded the PDF, told GPT 5.4 who the audience was, and asked for help. 

It immediately identified that the narrative was confusing + gave slide-by-slide edits.

How to Unlock a "Stuck" AMT Credit Balance (Faster)

An easy way to become a prolific writer:

1. Write & publish 1 Article on X for 7 days in a row
2. Review your analytics—what topic worked best?
3. Rewrite that same topic for 5 different audiences.
4. Repeat.

You will never run out of ideas.

Ever.

TUDCA is NOT just for liver detox, it's a highly effective neuro-protective aid.

TUDCA seems like a tool that is very targeted towards just improving 'detox' because it is a bile acid that should serve the sole purpose of improving bile flow

until you see that it actually has

I've said this very often

but dialing in on mineral metabolism is truly one of the highest ROI things you can do for health/performance

- 1g elemental magnesium
- maintaining a good calcium:phosphate of at least 1:1
- eating oysters
- getting in some boron
- not neglecting

(very) simple heavy metal detox tools, that WORK

- boron 3mg
- tamarind fruit/paste
- nettle tea/fiji water
- sunlight (melanin is a chelation agent)
- apolactoferrin (if iron is high)
- more calcium, magnesium and zinc
- enterosgel
- TUDCA (enhanced excretion through bile)

Reverse dieting is bullshit 

You don’t gain fat at maintenance 

But when you increase calories by 400-500… you gain some water weight, glycogen. And scale goes up.

People wrongfully think this is fat gain. It’s not.

Goal weight x 13 calories.

Goal weight x 0.8 protein in grams 

12k steps daily.

2-3 heavy lifts per week (ripped artiste in Kinobody app)

That is the formula 

If u want to go more aggressive use 12. But I prefer 13x goal weight.

The only reason you don’t get chiseled on

I didn’t even drop my calories on tirzep…. It simply allowed me to improve adherence to my protocol. 

Goal weight x 13…

170 x 13 =2,200

I’ve been doing 2200….. but actually hitting that consistently. 

In the past I’d aim for about 2300-2400… and often be closer to

Yess there gets a point where cutting below a certain body fat level is like pulling teeth.

Leptin levels are proportionate to body fat. As fat levels go down, leptin levels drop.

This influences appetite and energy expenditure. 

This is often why we tend to hit sticking

This is what I’ve been saying. Glucagon is catabolic. Raises energy expenditure, not inherently muscle sparing.

Now LBM, goes up a few pounds after bringing calories up.

But the whole reta is way better for muscle retention vs tirzep is wrong
